61: class AsyncIteratorWrapper:
62:     def __init__(self, obj):
63:         self._it = iter(obj)
64: 
65:     def __aiter__(self):
66:         return self
67: 
68:     async def __anext__(self):
69:         try:
70:             value = await self.next_async()
71:         except StopAsyncIteration:
72:             return
73:         return value
74: 
75:     @sync_to_async
76:     def next_async(self):
77:         try:
78:             return next(self._it)
79:         except StopIteration:
80:             raise StopAsyncIteration

83: def is_none_or_empty(item):
84:     return item is None or (hasattr(item, "__iter__") and len(item) == 0) or item == ""

86: 
87: def to_snake_case_from_dash(item: str):
88:     return item.replace("_", "-")

91: def get_absolute_path(filepath: Union[str, Path]) -> str:
92:     return str(Path(filepath).expanduser().absolute())
93: 
94: 
95: def resolve_absolute_path(filepath: Union[str, Optional[Path]], strict=False) -> Path:
96:     return Path(filepath).expanduser().absolute().resolve(strict=strict)

99: def get_from_dict(dictionary, *args):
100:     """null-aware get from a nested dictionary
101:     Returns: dictionary[args[0]][args[1]]... or None if any keys missing"""
102:     current = dictionary
103:     for arg in args:
104:         if not hasattr(current, "__iter__") or arg not in current:
105:             return None
106:         current = current[arg]
107:     return current
108: 
109: 
110: def merge_dicts(priority_dict: dict, default_dict: dict):
111:     merged_dict = priority_dict.copy()
112:     for key, _ in default_dict.items():
113:         if key not in priority_dict:
114:             merged_dict[key] = default_dict[key]
115:         elif isinstance(priority_dict[key], dict) and isinstance(default_dict[key], dict):
116:             merged_dict[key] = merge_dicts(priority_dict[key], default_dict[key])
117:     return merged_dict
118:

120: def fix_json_dict(json_dict: dict) -> dict:
121:     for k, v in json_dict.items():
122:         if v == "True" or v == "False":
123:             json_dict[k] = v == "True"
124:         if isinstance(v, dict):
125:             json_dict[k] = fix_json_dict(v)
126:     return json_dict

129: def get_file_type(file_type: str, file_content: bytes) -> tuple[str, str]:
130:     "Get file type from file mime type"
131: 
132:     # Extract encoding from file_type
133:     encoding = file_type.split("=")[1].strip().lower() if ";" in file_type else None
134:     file_type = file_type.split(";")[0].strip() if ";" in file_type else file_type
135: 
136:     # Infer content type from reading file content
137:     try:
138:         content_group = magika.identify_bytes(file_content).output.group
139:     except Exception:
140:         # Fallback to using just file type if content type cannot be inferred
141:         content_group = "unknown"
142: 
143:     if file_type in ["text/markdown"]:
144:         return "markdown", encoding
145:     elif file_type in ["text/org"]:
146:         return "org", encoding
147:     elif file_type in ["application/pdf"]:
148:         return "pdf", encoding
149:     elif file_type in ["application/msword", "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document"]:
150:         return "docx", encoding
151:     elif file_type in ["image/jpeg"]:
152:         return "image", encoding
153:     elif file_type in ["image/png"]:
154:         return "image", encoding
155:     elif file_type in ["image/webp"]:
156:         return "image", encoding
157:     elif content_group in ["code", "text"]:
158:         return "plaintext", encoding
159:     else:
160:         return "other", encoding

163: def load_model(
164:     model_name: str, model_type, model_dir=None, device: str = None
165: ) -> Union[BaseEncoder, SentenceTransformer, CrossEncoder]:
166:     "Load model from disk or huggingface"
167:     # Construct model path
168:     logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
169:     model_path = path.join(model_dir, model_name.replace("/", "_")) if model_dir is not None else None
170: 
171:     # Load model from model_path if it exists there
172:     model_type_class = get_class_by_name(model_type) if isinstance(model_type, str) else model_type
173:     if model_path is not None and resolve_absolute_path(model_path).exists():
174:         logger.debug(f"Loading {model_name} model from disk")
175:         model = model_type_class(get_absolute_path(model_path), device=device)
176:     # Else load the model from the model_name
177:     else:
178:         logger.info(f"🤖 Downloading {model_name} model from web")
179:         model = model_type_class(model_name, device=device)
180:         if model_path is not None:
181:             logger.info(f"📩 Saved {model_name} model to disk")
182:             model.save(model_path)
183: 
184:     return model
185:

187: def get_class_by_name(name: str) -> object:
188:     "Returns the class object from name string"
189:     module_name, class_name = name.rsplit(".", 1)
190:     return getattr(import_module(module_name), class_name)

193: class timer:
194:     """Context manager to log time taken for a block of code to run"""
195: 
196:     def __init__(self, message: str, logger: logging.Logger, device: torch.device = None, log_level=logging.DEBUG):
197:         self.message = message
198:         self.logger = logger.debug if log_level == logging.DEBUG else logger.info
199:         self.device = device
200: 
201:     def __enter__(self):
202:         self.start = perf_counter()
203:         return self
204: 
205:     def __exit__(self, *_):
206:         elapsed = perf_counter() - self.start
207:         if self.device is None:
208:             self.logger(f"{self.message}: {elapsed:.3f} seconds")
209:         else:
210:             self.logger(f"{self.message}: {elapsed:.3f} seconds on device: {self.device}")
211:

213: class LRU(OrderedDict):
214:     def __init__(self, *args, capacity=128, **kwargs):
215:         self.capacity = capacity
216:         super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
217: 
218:     def __getitem__(self, key):
219:         value = super().__getitem__(key)
220:         self.move_to_end(key)
221:         return value
222: 
223:     def __setitem__(self, key, value):
224:         super().__setitem__(key, value)
225:         if len(self) > self.capacity:
226:             oldest = next(iter(self))
227:             del self[oldest]

230: def get_server_id():
231:     """Get, Generate Persistent, Random ID per server install.
232:     Helps count distinct khoj servers deployed.
233:     Maintains anonymity by using non-PII random id."""
234:     # Initialize server_id to None
235:     server_id = None
236:     # Expand path to the khoj env file. It contains persistent internal app data
237:     app_env_filename = path.expanduser(constants.app_env_filepath)
238: 
239:     # Check if the file exists
240:     if path.exists(app_env_filename):
241:         # Read the contents of the file
242:         with open(app_env_filename, "r") as f:
243:             contents = f.readlines()
244: 
245:         # Extract the server_id from the contents
246:         for line in contents:
247:             key, value = line.strip().split("=")
248:             if key.strip() == "server_id":
249:                 server_id = value.strip()
250:                 break
251: 
252:         # If server_id is not found, generate and write to env file
253:         if server_id is None:
254:             # If server_id is not found, generate a new one
255:             server_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
256: 
257:             with open(app_env_filename, "a") as f:
258:                 f.write("server_id=" + server_id + "\n")
259:     else:
260:         # If server_id is not found, generate a new one
261:         server_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
262: 
263:         # Create khoj config directory if it doesn't exist
264:         os.makedirs(path.dirname(app_env_filename), exist_ok=True)
265: 
266:         # Write the server_id to the env file
267:         with open(app_env_filename, "w") as f:
268:             f.write("server_id=" + server_id + "\n")
269: 
270:     return server_id
271:

273: def log_telemetry(
274:     telemetry_type: str,
275:     api: str = None,
276:     client: Optional[str] = None,
277:     disable_telemetry_env: bool = False,
278:     properties: dict = None,
279: ):
280:     """Log basic app usage telemetry like client, os, api called"""
281:     # Do not log usage telemetry, if telemetry is disabled via app config
282:     if disable_telemetry_env:
283:         return []
284: 
285:     if properties.get("server_id") is None:
286:         properties["server_id"] = get_server_id()
287: 
288:     # Populate telemetry data to log
289:     request_body = {
290:         "telemetry_type": telemetry_type,
291:         "server_version": version("khoj"),
292:         "os": platform.system(),
293:         "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),
294:     }
295:     request_body.update(properties or {})
296:     if api:
297:         # API endpoint on server called by client
298:         request_body["api"] = api
299:     if client:
300:         # Client from which the API was called. E.g. Emacs, Obsidian
301:         request_body["client"] = client
302: 
303:     # Log telemetry data to telemetry endpoint
304:     return request_body

307: def get_device_memory() -> int:
308:     """Get device memory in GB"""
309:     device = get_device()
310:     if device.type == "cuda":
311:         return torch.cuda.get_device_properties(device).total_memory
312:     elif device.type == "mps":
313:         return torch.mps.driver_allocated_memory()
314:     else:
315:         return psutil.virtual_memory().total
316: 
317: 
318: def get_device() -> torch.device:
319:     """Get device to run model on"""
320:     if torch.cuda.is_available():
321:         # Use CUDA GPU
322:         return torch.device("cuda:0")
323:     elif torch.backends.mps.is_available():
324:         # Use Apple M1 Metal Acceleration
325:         return torch.device("mps")
326:     else:
327:         return torch.device("cpu")

332:     Set E2B_API_KEY environment variable to use it."""
333:     return not is_none_or_empty(os.getenv("E2B_API_KEY"))

336: class ToolDefinition:
337:     def __init__(self, name: str, description: str, schema: dict):
338:         self.name = name
339:         self.description = description
340:         self.schema = schema
341: 
342: 
343: def create_tool_definition(
344:     schema: Type[BaseModel],
345:     name: str = None,
346:     description: Optional[str] = None,
347: ) -> ToolDefinition:
348:     """
349:     Converts a response schema BaseModel class into a normalized tool definition.
350: 
351:     A standard AI provider agnostic tool format to specify tools the model can use.
352:     Common logic used across models is kept here. AI provider specific adaptations
353:     should be handled in provider code.
354: 
355:     Args:
356:         response_schema: The Pydantic BaseModel class to convert.
357:                          This class defines the response schema for the tool.
358:         tool_name: The name for the AI model tool (e.g., "get_weather", "plan_next_step").
359:         tool_description: Optional description for the AI model tool.
360:                            If None, it attempts to use the Pydantic model's docstring.
361:                            If that's also missing, a fallback description is generated.
362: 
363:     Returns:
364:         A normalized tool definition for AI model APIs.
365:     """
366:     raw_schema_dict = schema.model_json_schema()
367: 
368:     name = name or schema.__name__.lower()
369:     description = description
370:     if description is None:
371:         docstring = schema.__doc__
372:         if docstring:
373:             description = dedent(docstring).strip()
374:         else:
375:             # Fallback description if no explicit one or docstring is provided
376:             description = f"Tool named '{name}' accepts specified parameters."
377: 
378:     # Process properties to inline enums and remove $defs dependency
379:     processed_properties = {}
380:     original_properties = raw_schema_dict.get("properties", {})
381:     defs = raw_schema_dict.get("$defs", {})
382: 
383:     for prop_name, prop_schema in original_properties.items():
384:         current_prop_schema = deepcopy(prop_schema)  # Work on a copy
385:         # Check for enums defined directly in the property for simpler direct enum definitions.
386:         if "$ref" in current_prop_schema:
387:             ref_path = current_prop_schema["$ref"]
388:             if ref_path.startswith("#/$defs/"):
389:                 def_name = ref_path.split("/")[-1]
390:                 if def_name in defs and "enum" in defs[def_name]:
391:                     enum_def = defs[def_name]
392:                     current_prop_schema["enum"] = enum_def["enum"]
393:                     current_prop_schema["type"] = enum_def.get("type", "string")
394:                     if "description" not in current_prop_schema and "description" in enum_def:
395:                         current_prop_schema["description"] = enum_def["description"]
396:                     del current_prop_schema["$ref"]  # Remove the $ref as it's been inlined
397: 
398:         processed_properties[prop_name] = current_prop_schema
399: 
400:     # Generate the compiled schema dictionary for the tool definition.
401:     compiled_schema = {
402:         "type": "object",
403:         "properties": processed_properties,
404:         # Generate content in the order in which the schema properties were defined
405:         "property_ordering": list(schema.model_fields.keys()),
406:     }
407: 
408:     # Include 'required' fields if specified in the Pydantic model
409:     if "required" in raw_schema_dict and raw_schema_dict["required"]:
410:         compiled_schema["required"] = raw_schema_dict["required"]
411: 
412:     return ToolDefinition(name=name, description=description, schema=compiled_schema)
413:

616:         },
617:     ),
618:     ConversationCommand.SemanticSearchFiles: ToolDefinition(
619:         name="semantic_search_files",
620:         description=dedent(
621:             """
622:             To have the tool AI semantic search through the user's knowledge base.
623:             Helpful to answer questions for which finding some relevant notes or documents can be useful. Example: "When was Tom born?"
624:             This tool AI cannot find all relevant notes or documents, only a subset of them.
625:             It is a good starting point to find keywords, discover similar topics or related concepts and some relevant notes or documents.
626:             For a given query, the tool AI can perform a maximum of {max_search_queries} semantic search subqueries per iteration.
627:             """
628:         ).strip(),
629:         schema={
630:             "type": "object",
631:             "properties": {
632:                 "q": {
633:                     "type": "string",
634:                     "description": "Your natural language query for the tool to search in the user's knowledge base.",
635:                 },
636:             },
637:             "required": ["q"],
638:         },
639:     ),
640:     ConversationCommand.RegexSearchFiles: ToolDefinition(
641:         name="regex_search_files",
642:         description=dedent(
643:             """
644:             To search through the user's knowledge base using regex patterns. Returns all lines matching the pattern.
645:             Helpful to answer questions for which all relevant notes or documents are needed to complete the search. Example: "Notes that mention Tom".
646:             You need to know all the correct keywords or regex patterns for this tool to be useful.
647: 
648:             IMPORTANT:
649:             - The regex pattern will ONLY match content on a single line. Multi-line matches are NOT supported (even if you use \\n).
650: 
651:             TIPS:
652:             - The output follows a grep-like format. Matches are prefixed with the file path and line number. Useful to combine with viewing file around specific line numbers.
653: 
654:             An optional path prefix can restrict search to specific files/directories.
655:             Use lines_before, lines_after to show context around matches.
656:             """
657:         ).strip(),
658:         schema={
659:             "type": "object",
660:             "properties": {
661:                 "regex_pattern": {
662:                     "type": "string",
663:                     "description": "The regex pattern to search for content in the user's files.",
664:                 },
665:                 "path_prefix": {
666:                     "type": "string",
667:                     "description": "Optional path prefix to limit the search to files under a specified path.",
668:                 },
669:                 "lines_before": {
670:                     "type": "integer",
671:                     "description": "Optional number of lines to show before each line match for context. It should be a positive number between 0 and 20.",
672:                     "minimum": 0,
673:                     "maximum": 20,
674:                 },
675:                 "lines_after": {
676:                     "type": "integer",
677:                     "description": "Optional number of lines to show after each line match for context. It should be a positive number between 0 and 20.",
678:                     "minimum": 0,
679:                     "maximum": 20,
680:                 },
681:             },
682:             "required": ["regex_pattern"],
683:         },
684:     ),
685: }

713: def generate_random_name():
714:     # List of adjectives and nouns to choose from
715:     adjectives = [
716:         "happy",
717:         "serendipitous",
718:         "exuberant",
719:         "calm",
720:         "brave",
721:         "scared",
722:         "energetic",
723:         "chivalrous",
724:         "kind",
725:         "suave",
726:     ]
727:     nouns = ["dog", "cat", "falcon", "whale", "turtle", "rabbit", "hamster", "snake", "spider", "elephant"]
728: 
729:     # Select two random words from the lists
730:     adjective = random.choice(adjectives)
731:     noun = random.choice(nouns)
732: 
733:     # Combine the words to form a name
734:     name = f"{adjective} {noun}"
735: 
736:     return name
737:

740:     random_name = generate_random_name()
741: 
742:     random_name = random_name.replace(" ", "_")
743: 
744:     random_number = random.randint(1000, 9999)
745: 
746:     name = f"{random_name}{random_number}"
747: 
748:     return name

751: def batcher(iterable, max_n):
752:     "Split an iterable into chunks of size max_n"
753:     it = iter(iterable)
754:     while True:
755:         chunk = list(islice(it, max_n))
756:         if not chunk:
757:             return
758:         yield (x for x in chunk if x is not None)

824: def is_internal_url(url: str) -> bool:
825:     """
826:     Check if a URL is likely to be internal/non-public.
827: 
828:     Args:
829:     url (str): The URL to check.
830: 
831:     Returns:
832:     bool: True if the URL is likely internal, False otherwise.
833:     """
834:     try:
835:         parsed_url = urllib.parse.urlparse(url)
836:         hostname = parsed_url.hostname
837: 
838:         # Check for localhost
839:         if hostname in ["localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"]:
840:             return True
841: 
842:         # Check for IP addresses in private ranges
843:         try:
844:             ip = ipaddress.ip_address(hostname)
845:             return ip.is_private
846:         except ValueError:
847:             pass  # Not an IP address, continue with other checks
848: 
849:         # Check for common internal TLDs
850:         internal_tlds = [".local", ".internal", ".private", ".corp", ".home", ".lan"]
851:         if any(hostname.endswith(tld) for tld in internal_tlds):
852:             return True
853: 
854:         # Check for URLs without a TLD
855:         if "." not in hostname:
856:             return True
857: 
858:         return False
859:     except Exception:
860:         # If we can't parse the URL or something else goes wrong, assume it's not internal
861:         return False
862:

864: def convert_image_to_webp(image_bytes):
865:     """Convert image bytes to webp format for faster loading"""
866:     image_io = io.BytesIO(image_bytes)
867:     with Image.open(image_io) as original_image:
868:         webp_image_io = io.BytesIO()
869:         original_image.save(webp_image_io, "WEBP")
870: 
871:         # Encode the WebP image back to base64
872:         webp_image_bytes = webp_image_io.getvalue()
873:         webp_image_io.close()
874:         return webp_image_bytes
875: 
876: 
877: def convert_image_data_uri(image_data_uri: str, target_format: str = "png") -> str:
878:     """
879:     Convert image (in data URI) to target format.
880: 
881:     Target format can be png, jpg, webp etc.
882:     Returns the converted image as a data URI.
883:     """
884:     base64_data = image_data_uri.split(",", 1)[1]
885:     image_type = image_data_uri.split(";")[0].split(":")[1].split("/")[1]
886:     if image_type.lower() == target_format.lower():
887:         return image_data_uri
888: 
889:     image_bytes = base64.b64decode(base64_data)
890:     image_io = io.BytesIO(image_bytes)
891:     with Image.open(image_io) as original_image:
892:         output_image_io = io.BytesIO()
893:         original_image.save(output_image_io, target_format.upper())
894: 
895:         # Encode the image back to base64
896:         output_image_bytes = output_image_io.getvalue()
897:         output_image_io.close()
898:         output_base64_data = base64.b64encode(output_image_bytes).decode("utf-8")
899:         output_data_uri = f"data:image/{target_format};base64,{output_base64_data}"
900:         return output_data_uri
901:

909: def truncate_code_context(original_code_results: dict[str, Any], max_chars=10000) -> dict[str, Any]:
910:     """
911:     Truncate large output files and drop image file data from code results.
912:     """
913:     # Create a deep copy of the code results to avoid modifying the original data
914:     code_results = copy.deepcopy(original_code_results)
915:     for code_result in code_results.values():
916:         for idx, output_file in enumerate(code_result["results"]["output_files"]):
917:             # Drop image files from code results
918:             if Path(output_file["filename"]).suffix in {".png", ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".webp"}:
919:                 code_result["results"]["output_files"][idx] = {
920:                     "filename": output_file["filename"],
921:                     "b64_data": "[placeholder for generated image data for brevity]",
922:                 }
923:             # Truncate large output files
924:             elif len(output_file["b64_data"]) > max_chars:
925:                 code_result["results"]["output_files"][idx] = {
926:                     "filename": output_file["filename"],
927:                     "b64_data": output_file["b64_data"][:max_chars] + "...",
928:                 }
929:         # Truncate long "words" in stdout, stderr. Words are alphanumeric strings not separated by whitespace.
930:         for key in ["std_out", "std_err"]:
931:             if key in code_result["results"]:
932:                 code_result["results"][key] = re.sub(
933:                     r"\S{1000,}", lambda m: m.group(0)[:1000] + "...", code_result["results"][key]
934:                 )
935: 
936:     return code_results
937:

939: @lru_cache
940: def tz_to_cc_map() -> dict[str, str]:
941:     """Create a mapping of timezone to country code"""
942:     timezone_country = {}
943:     for countrycode in country_timezones:
944:         timezones = country_timezones[countrycode]
945:         for timezone in timezones:
946:             timezone_country[timezone] = countrycode
947:     return timezone_country
948: 
949: 
950: def get_country_code_from_timezone(tz: str) -> str:
951:     """Get country code from timezone"""
952:     return tz_to_cc_map().get(tz, "US")
953: 
954: 
955: def get_country_name_from_timezone(tz: str) -> str:
956:     """Get country name from timezone"""
957:     return country_names.get(get_country_code_from_timezone(tz), "United States")

959: 
960: def get_cost_of_chat_message(
961:     model_name: str,
962:     input_tokens: int = 0,
963:     output_tokens: int = 0,
964:     thought_tokens: int = 0,
965:     cache_read_tokens: int = 0,
966:     cache_write_tokens: int = 0,
967:     prev_cost: float = 0.0,
968: ):
969:     """
970:     Calculate cost of chat message based on input and output tokens
971:     """
972: 
973:     # Calculate cost of input and output tokens. Costs are per million tokens
974:     input_cost = constants.model_to_cost.get(model_name, {}).get("input", 0) * (input_tokens / 1e6)
975:     output_cost = constants.model_to_cost.get(model_name, {}).get("output", 0) * (output_tokens / 1e6)
976:     thought_cost = constants.model_to_cost.get(model_name, {}).get("thought", 0) * (thought_tokens / 1e6)
977:     cache_read_cost = constants.model_to_cost.get(model_name, {}).get("cache_read", 0) * (cache_read_tokens / 1e6)
978:     cache_write_cost = constants.model_to_cost.get(model_name, {}).get("cache_write", 0) * (cache_write_tokens / 1e6)
979: 
980:     return input_cost + output_cost + thought_cost + cache_read_cost + cache_write_cost + prev_cost
981: 
982: 
983: def get_encoder(
984:     model_name: str,
985:     tokenizer_name=None,
986: ) -> tiktoken.Encoding | PreTrainedTokenizer | PreTrainedTokenizerFast:
987:     default_tokenizer = "gpt-4o"
988: 
989:     try:
990:         if tokenizer_name:
991:             encoder = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(tokenizer_name)
992:         else:
993:             # as tiktoken doesn't recognize o1 model series yet
994:             encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model("gpt-4o" if model_name.startswith("o1") else model_name)
995:     except Exception:
996:         encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(default_tokenizer)
997:     return encoder
998: 
999: 
1000: def count_tokens(
1001:     message_content: str | list[str | dict],
1002:     encoder: PreTrainedTokenizer | PreTrainedTokenizerFast | tiktoken.Encoding,
1003: ) -> int:
1004:     """
1005:     Count the total number of tokens in a list of messages.
1006: 
1007:     Assumes each images takes 500 tokens for approximation.
1008:     """
1009:     if isinstance(message_content, list):
1010:         image_count = 0
1011:         message_content_parts: list[str] = []
1012:         # Collate message content into single string to ease token counting
1013:         for part in message_content:
1014:             if isinstance(part, dict) and part.get("type") == "image_url":
1015:                 image_count += 1
1016:             elif isinstance(part, dict) and part.get("type") == "text":
1017:                 message_content_parts.append(part["text"])
1018:             elif isinstance(part, dict) and hasattr(part, "model_dump"):
1019:                 message_content_parts.append(json.dumps(part.model_dump()))
1020:             elif isinstance(part, dict) and hasattr(part, "__dict__"):
1021:                 message_content_parts.append(json.dumps(part.__dict__))
1022:             elif isinstance(part, dict):
1023:                 # If part is a dict but not a recognized type, convert to JSON string
1024:                 try:
1025:                     # Skip non-serializable binary values for token counting
1026:                     serializable_part = {
1027:                         k: v for k, v in part.items() if not isinstance(v, (bytes, bytearray, memoryview))
1028:                     }
1029:                     message_content_parts.append(json.dumps(serializable_part))
1030:                 except (TypeError, ValueError) as e:
1031:                     logger.warning(
1032:                         f"Failed to serialize part {part} to JSON. Assume its an image for token counting.\n{e}."
1033:                     )
1034:                     image_count += 1  # Treat as an image/binary if serialization fails
1035:             elif isinstance(part, str):
1036:                 message_content_parts.append(part)
1037:             else:
1038:                 logger.warning(f"Unknown message type: {part}. Skip for token counting.")
1039:         message_content = "\n".join(message_content_parts).rstrip()
1040:         return len(encoder.encode(message_content)) + image_count * 500
1041:     elif isinstance(message_content, str):
1042:         return len(encoder.encode(message_content))
1043:     else:
1044:         return len(encoder.encode(json.dumps(message_content)))
1045: 
1046: 
1047: def get_chat_usage_metrics(
1048:     model_name: str,
1049:     input_tokens: int = 0,
1050:     output_tokens: int = 0,
1051:     cache_read_tokens: int = 0,
1052:     cache_write_tokens: int = 0,
1053:     thought_tokens: int = 0,
1054:     usage: dict = {},
1055:     cost: float = None,
1056: ):
1057:     """
1058:     Get usage metrics for chat message based on input and output tokens and cost
1059:     """
1060:     prev_usage = usage or {
1061:         "input_tokens": 0,
1062:         "output_tokens": 0,
1063:         "thought_tokens": 0,
1064:         "cache_read_tokens": 0,
1065:         "cache_write_tokens": 0,
1066:         "cost": 0.0,
1067:     }
1068:     current_usage = {
1069:         "input_tokens": prev_usage["input_tokens"] + input_tokens,
1070:         "output_tokens": prev_usage["output_tokens"] + output_tokens,
1071:         "thought_tokens": prev_usage.get("thought_tokens", 0) + thought_tokens,
1072:         "cache_read_tokens": prev_usage.get("cache_read_tokens", 0) + cache_read_tokens,
1073:         "cache_write_tokens": prev_usage.get("cache_write_tokens", 0) + cache_write_tokens,
1074:         "cost": cost
1075:         or get_cost_of_chat_message(
1076:             model_name,
1077:             input_tokens,
1078:             output_tokens,
1079:             thought_tokens,
1080:             cache_read_tokens,
1081:             cache_write_tokens,
1082:             prev_cost=prev_usage["cost"],
1083:         ),
1084:     }
1085:     logger.debug(f"AI API usage by {model_name}: {current_usage}")
1086:     return current_usage
1087:

1089: class AiApiInfo(NamedTuple):
1090:     region: str
1091:     project: str
1092:     credentials: Credentials
1093:     api_key: str
1094: 
1095: 
1096: def get_gcp_credentials(credentials_b64: str) -> Optional[Credentials]:
1097:     """
1098:     Get GCP credentials from base64 encoded service account credentials json keyfile
1099:     """
1100:     credentials_json = base64.b64decode(credentials_b64).decode("utf-8")
1101:     credentials_dict = pyjson5.loads(credentials_json)
1102:     credentials = service_account.Credentials.from_service_account_info(credentials_dict)
1103:     return credentials.with_scopes(scopes=["https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"])
1104: 
1105: 
1106: def get_gcp_project_info(parsed_api_url: ParseResult) -> Tuple[str, str]:
1107:     """
1108:     Extract region, project id from GCP API url
1109:     API url is of form https://{REGION}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{PROJECT_ID}...
1110:     """
1111:     # Extract region from hostname
1112:     hostname = parsed_api_url.netloc
1113:     region = hostname.split("-aiplatform")[0] if "-aiplatform" in hostname else ""
1114: 
1115:     # Extract project ID from path (e.g., "/v1/projects/my-project/...")
1116:     path_parts = parsed_api_url.path.split("/")
1117:     project_id = ""
1118:     for i, part in enumerate(path_parts):
1119:         if part == "projects" and i + 1 < len(path_parts):
1120:             project_id = path_parts[i + 1]
1121:             break
1122: 
1123:     return region, project_id
1124: 
1125: 
1126: def get_ai_api_info(api_key, api_base_url: str = None) -> AiApiInfo:
1127:     """
1128:     Get the GCP Vertex or default AI API client info based on the API key and URL.
1129:     """
1130:     region, project_id, credentials = None, None, None
1131:     # Check if AI model to be used via GCP Vertex API
1132:     parsed_api_url = urlparse(api_base_url)
1133:     if parsed_api_url.hostname and parsed_api_url.hostname.endswith(".googleapis.com"):
1134:         region, project_id = get_gcp_project_info(parsed_api_url)
1135:         credentials = get_gcp_credentials(api_key)
1136:     if credentials:
1137:         api_key = None
1138:     return AiApiInfo(region=region, project=project_id, credentials=credentials, api_key=api_key)
1139: 
1140: 
1141: def get_openai_client(api_key: str, api_base_url: str) -> Union[openai.OpenAI, openai.AzureOpenAI]:
1142:     """Get OpenAI or AzureOpenAI client based on the API Base URL"""
1143:     parsed_url = urlparse(api_base_url)
1144:     if parsed_url.hostname and parsed_url.hostname.endswith(".openai.azure.com"):
1145:         client = openai.AzureOpenAI(
1146:             api_key=api_key,
1147:             azure_endpoint=api_base_url,
1148:             api_version="2024-10-21",
1149:         )
1150:     else:
1151:         client = openai.OpenAI(
1152:             api_key=api_key,
1153:             base_url=api_base_url,
1154:         )
1155:     return client
1156: 
1157: 
1158: def get_openai_async_client(api_key: str, api_base_url: str) -> Union[openai.AsyncOpenAI, openai.AsyncAzureOpenAI]:
1159:     """Get OpenAI or AzureOpenAI client based on the API Base URL"""
1160:     parsed_url = urlparse(api_base_url)
1161:     if parsed_url.hostname and parsed_url.hostname.endswith(".openai.azure.com"):
1162:         client = openai.AsyncAzureOpenAI(
1163:             api_key=api_key,
1164:             azure_endpoint=api_base_url,
1165:             api_version="2024-10-21",
1166:         )
1167:     else:
1168:         client = openai.AsyncOpenAI(
1169:             api_key=api_key,
1170:             base_url=api_base_url,
1171:         )
1172:     return client
1173: 
1174: 
1175: def get_anthropic_client(api_key, api_base_url=None) -> anthropic.Anthropic | anthropic.AnthropicVertex:
1176:     api_info = get_ai_api_info(api_key, api_base_url)
1177:     if api_info.api_key:
1178:         client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_info.api_key)
1179:     else:
1180:         client = anthropic.AnthropicVertex(
1181:             region=api_info.region,
1182:             project_id=api_info.project,
1183:             credentials=api_info.credentials,
1184:         )
1185:     return client

1212: def normalize_email(email: str, check_deliverability=False) -> tuple[str, bool]:
1213:     """Normalize, validate and check deliverability of email address"""
1214:     lower_email = email.lower()
1215:     try:
1216:         valid_email = validate_email(lower_email, check_deliverability=check_deliverability)
1217:         return valid_email.normalized, True
1218:     except (EmailNotValidError, EmailUndeliverableError):
1219:         return lower_email, False
1220: 
1221: 
1222: def clean_text_for_db(text):
1223:     """Remove characters that PostgreSQL DB cannot store in text fields.
1224: 
1225:     PostgreSQL text fields cannot contain NUL (0x00) characters.
1226:     This is a database-level constraint.
1227:     """
1228:     if not isinstance(text, str):
1229:         return text
1230:     return text.replace("\x00", "")
1231: 
1232: 
1233: def clean_object_for_db(data):
1234:     """Recursively clean PostgreSQL-incompatible characters from nested data structures."""
1235:     if isinstance(data, str):
1236:         return clean_text_for_db(data)
1237:     elif isinstance(data, dict):
1238:         return {k: clean_object_for_db(v) for k, v in data.items()}
1239:     elif isinstance(data, list):
1240:         return [clean_object_for_db(item) for item in data]
1241:     else:
1242:         return data
1243: 
1244: 
1245: def dict_to_tuple(d):
1246:     # Recursively convert dicts to sorted tuples for hashability
1247:     if isinstance(d, dict):
1248:         return tuple(sorted((k, dict_to_tuple(v)) for k, v in d.items()))
1249:     elif isinstance(d, list):
1250:         return tuple(dict_to_tuple(i) for i in d)
1251:     else:
1252:         return d
1253: 
1254: 
1255: def rrf_fuse(results_lists: List[List[dict]], k: int = 60, limit: int = 10) -> List[dict]:
1256:     """
1257:     Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) for combining multiple ranked result lists.
1258: 
1259:     RRF formula: score = sum(1 / (k + rank + 1)) for each result list
1260:     Results are deduplicated by ID and sorted by final RRF score descending.
1261: 
1262:     Args:
1263:         results_lists: List of result lists, where each result is a dict with 'id' or 'entry' key
1264:         k: RRF constant (default 60), higher values give more weight to lower-ranked items
1265:         limit: Maximum number of results to return (default 10)
1266: 
1267:     Returns:
1268:         List of fused results sorted by RRF score descending, limited to `limit` items
1269:     """
1270:     if not results_lists:
1271:         return []
1272: 
1273:     # Track RRF scores and result data by unique ID
1274:     scores: dict[str, float] = {}
1275:     results_by_id: dict[str, dict] = {}
1276: 
1277:     for results in results_lists:
1278:         if not results:
1279:             continue
1280: 
1281:         for rank, result in enumerate(results):
1282:             # Get unique identifier for the result
1283:             result_id = result.get("id") or result.get("entry")
1284:             if result_id is None:
1285:                 continue  # Skip results without identifiable ID
1286: 
1287:             # Convert ID to string for consistent hashing
1288:             result_id = str(result_id)
1289: 
1290:             # Calculate RRF contribution: 1 / (k + rank + 1)
1291:             rrf_score = 1.0 / (k + rank + 1)
1292: 
1293:             # Accumulate score across all result lists
1294:             scores[result_id] = scores.get(result_id, 0.0) + rrf_score
1295: 
1296:             # Store the result data (prefer first occurrence)
1297:             if result_id not in results_by_id:
1298:                 results_by_id[result_id] = result
1299: 
1300:     if not scores:
1301:         return []
1302: 
1303:     # Sort by RRF score descending and return top `limit` results
1304:     sorted_ids = sorted(scores.keys(), key=lambda x: scores[x], reverse=True)
1305:     top_ids = sorted_ids[:limit]
1306: 
1307:     # Return results in sorted order with their RRF scores included
1308:     fused_results = []
1309:     for result_id in top_ids:
1310:         result = results_by_id[result_id].copy()
1311:         result["rrf_score"] = scores[result_id]
1312:         fused_results.append(result)
1313: 
1314:     return fused_results
